title: DevHarness Gate Attack Verdict Output
description: Output contract for DevHarness attack gates that approve, request revision, or block an upstream draft.

outcome.description: Machine-readable stage outcome used by workflow routing.
outcome.x-usage: Downstream next/match logic reads this value; choose only the enum/const value that matches the stage state.

summary.description: Concise human-readable summary for this contract section.

evidence_checked.description: Sources, artifacts, commands, or state inspected before issuing the verdict.
evidence_checked.x-usage: Keep this concrete so downstream revision or review can see what was actually checked.

findings.description: Actionable findings, concerns, or reasons behind the verdict.
findings.x-usage: Use empty only when the verdict is clean; include enough detail for the next worker to fix or accept the decision.

verdict.description: Structured decision payload for this gate.
verdict.x-usage: Revision routing uses this verdict to decide whether the draft can advance or must be revised before another attack.

artifacts.description: Optional structured artifacts emitted by this stage.
artifacts.x-usage: Use for named files or durable handoff artifacts the orchestrator should preserve.

results.description: Optional structured result summaries emitted by this stage.
results.x-usage: Use for compact machine-readable evidence or decisions that should remain in baton state.

summary.description: Concise human-readable summary for this contract section.

source_step_id.description: Workflow step id that discovered or owns the blocker.

needed.description: Specific missing input, decision, permission, or evidence needed to proceed.

evidence.description: Evidence references supporting the handoff, verdict, or blocker.

risk.description: Residual risk if the blocker or finding is ignored.

blocker.description: Blocker details required when the stage cannot produce a usable contract.
blocker.x-usage: Populate when outcome is blocked so the orchestrator can stop and ask for the missing input or decision.
